17	## from goal: list 2 commands in uv crate root (2026-05-10)
18	
19	`cache_clean` is one of the top-level command entry points exposed by the
20	`uv` crate (see [[uv-commands-overview]]). It clears the on-disk cache,
21	either fully or scoped to a list of package names.
22	
23	### Signature
24	
25	```rust
26	// from crates/uv/src/commands/cache_clean.rs
27	pub(crate) async fn cache_clean(
28	    packages: &[PackageName],
29	    force: bool,
30	    cache: Cache,
31	    printer: Printer,
32	) -> Result<ExitStatus>
33	```
34	
35	### Behavior outline
36	
37	- If the cache directory does not exist, prints a notice and returns
38	  `ExitStatus::Success` without doing any I/O.
39	- Acquires an exclusive lock on the cache. If another `uv` process holds
40	  the lock, the user can pass `--force` to bypass waiting; otherwise the
41	  command waits for the lock.
42	- With no `packages`, walks the whole cache and clears it via
43	  `cache.clear(...)`, reporting progress through `CleaningDirectoryReporter`.
44	- With one or more `packages`, calls `cache.remove(package)` per name and
45	  reports per-package progress via `CleaningPackageReporter`.
46	- Prints a final summary of files / directories removed and total bytes
47	  reclaimed (using `human_readable_bytes` from the parent `commands`
48	  module).
49	
50	### Re-export
51	
52	The function is re-exported from `crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs`:
53	
54	```rust
55	// from crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs
56	pub(crate) use cache_clean::cache_clean;
57	```
58	
59	So callers in the `uv` binary refer to it as
60	`crate::commands::cache_clean`.
61

---
title: cache_clean Command
type: module
sources:
  - path: crates/uv/src/commands/cache_clean.rs
  - path: crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs
goals:
  - "list 2 commands in uv crate root"
created: '2026-05-10'
updated: '2026-05-10'
related:
  - "[[venv]]"
  - "[[uv-commands-overview]]"
stale: false
---

## from goal: list 2 commands in uv crate root (2026-05-10)

`cache_clean` is one of the top-level command entry points exposed by the
`uv` crate (see [[uv-commands-overview]]). It clears the on-disk cache,
either fully or scoped to a list of package names.

### Signature

```rust
// from crates/uv/src/commands/cache_clean.rs
pub(crate) async fn cache_clean(
    packages: &[PackageName],
    force: bool,
    cache: Cache,
    printer: Printer,
) -> Result<ExitStatus>
```

### Behavior outline

- If the cache directory does not exist, prints a notice and returns
  `ExitStatus::Success` without doing any I/O.
- Acquires an exclusive lock on the cache. If another `uv` process holds
  the lock, the user can pass `--force` to bypass waiting; otherwise the
  command waits for the lock.
- With no `packages`, walks the whole cache and clears it via
  `cache.clear(...)`, reporting progress through `CleaningDirectoryReporter`.
- With one or more `packages`, calls `cache.remove(package)` per name and
  reports per-package progress via `CleaningPackageReporter`.
- Prints a final summary of files / directories removed and total bytes
  reclaimed (using `human_readable_bytes` from the parent `commands`
  module).

### Re-export

The function is re-exported from `crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs`:

```rust
// from crates/uv/src/commands/mod.rs
pub(crate) use cache_clean::cache_clean;
```

So callers in the `uv` binary refer to it as
`crate::commands::cache_clean`.

934:pub enum CacheBucket {
935-    /// Wheels (excluding built wheels), alongside their metadata and cache policy.
936-    ///
937-    \ There are three kinds from cache entries: Wheel metadata and policy as `MsgPack` files, the
938-    /// wheels themselves, and the unzipped wheel archives. If a wheel file is over an in-memory
939-    /// size threshold, we first download the zip file into the cache, then unzip it into a
940-    /// directory with the same name (exclusive of the `.whl` extension).
941-    ///
942-    \ Cache structure:
943-    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/pypi/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
944-    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/<digest(index-url)>/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
945-    ///  * `wheel-metadata-v0/url/<digest(url)>/foo/{foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.msgpack, foo-1.0.0-py3-none-any.whl}`
946-    ///
947-    \ See `uv_client::RegistryClient::wheel_metadata` for information on how wheel metadata
948-    /// is fetched.
949-    ///
950-    /// # Example
951-    ///
952-    \ Consider the following `requirements.in`:
953-    /// ```text
954-    /// # pypi wheel
955-    /// pandas
956-    /// # url wheel
957-    \ flask @ https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/36/42/015c23096649b908c809c69388a805a571a3bea44362fe87e33fc3afa01f/flask-3.0.0-py3-none-any.whl
958-    /// ```
959-    ///
960-    /// When we run `pip compile`, it will only fetch and cache the metadata (and cache policy), it
961-    \ doesn't need the actual wheels yet:
962-    /// ```text
963-    /// wheel-v0
964-    /// ├── pypi

content.text: uv 的快取以 `crates/uv-cache` 為核心，依用途切成多個 `CacheBucket`（例如 `wheel-v0`、`wheel-metadata-v0`、`archive-v0`、`built-wheels-v0` 等），每個 bucket 內再依來源（`pypi/`、`<digest(index-url)>/`、`url/<digest(url)>/`）做分片，最終以 `CacheShard`（資料夾）與 `CacheEntry`（檔案）兩個基本單位定位內容。寫入時透過 `LockedFile` 取得 per-entry 排他鎖以避免併發衝突，而高層命令如 [[cache-clean]] 則對整個 cache root 取鎖，再呼叫 `Cache::clear` 或 `Cache::remove(package)` 來全清或按套件清除。所有路徑都帶 `-v0` 之類的 schema 版本（見 `ARCHIVE_VERSION`），方便日後格式破壞性變更時直接換桶而非遷移舊資料。

result: uv 的快取以 `crates/uv-cache` 為核心，依用途切成多個 `CacheBucket`（例如 `wheel-v0`、`wheel-metadata-v0`、`archive-v0`、`built-wheels-v0` 等），每個 bucket 內再依來源（`pypi/`、`<digest(index-url)>/`、`url/<digest(url)>/`）做分片，最終以 `CacheShard`（資料夾）與 `CacheEntry`（檔案）兩個基本單位定位內容。寫入時透過 `LockedFile` 取得 per-entry 排他鎖以避免併發衝突，而高層命令如 [[cache-clean]] 則對整個 cache root 取鎖，再呼叫 `Cache::clear` 或 `Cache::remove(package)` 來全清或按套件清除。所有路徑都帶 `-v0` 之類的 schema 版本（見 `ARCHIVE_VERSION`），方便日後格式破壞性變更時直接換桶而非遷移舊資料。
